item.title: Mauritius weather bulletin – 24 March 2026
item.description: Weather news for Mauritius issued at 16h30 on Tuesday 24 March 2026.General situation:1. Few clouds coming from the Eastern sector will influence the local weather as from tonight.2. A light and warm airstream is flowing over the region.Forecast for the next 24 hours:Partly cloudy in general tonight and tomorrow morning becoming cloudy at times to the East and over the highgrounds with showers.The minimum temperature will be between 21 and 23 degrees Celsius over the central plateau and between 25 and 27 degrees Celsius along the coasts.Cloud developments are expected tomorrow afternoon over the Western part of the island and over the Central Plateau with localised showers. These showers may be temporarily moderate.Partly cloudy elsewhere.The maximum temperature will be slightly above the mean and will be between 26 and 29 degrees Celsius over the highgrounds and between 31 and 34 degrees Celsius over the coastal regions.Wind will blow from the Eastern sector at about 15 km/h.Sea moderate beyond the reefs with Southwesterly swells of the order of 2 metres.High tides :17h47 and tomorrow 04h04.Low tides :23h20 and tomorrow 12h28.Sunset :18h18.Sunrise (tomorrow) :06h15.Atmospheric pressure at 16h00:1013 hectopascals.

item.title: Ghana and EU sign landmark defence deal to combat militant Islamist threat
item.description: [BBC Africa] Ghana is the first African country to make such a deal with the EU, the Ghanaian vice-president says. [Africanews] The agreement, signed in Accra by EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas and Ghanaian Vice President Jane Naana Opoku-Agyemang, is aimed at strengthening cooperation in areas including counterterrorism, intelligence sharing and crisis response [Africanews] Ghana and the European Union on Tuesday signed their first formal defence partnership, as the country presents itself as a regional bulwark against the growing jihadist violence roiling west Africa.
item.titles: Ghana and EU sign landmark defence deal to combat militant Islamist threat Ghana, European Union agree defence pact Ghana, EU sign first pact to counter West Africa's growing insecurity

item.title: Israel says it will take control of large buffer zone in southern Lebanon
item.description: Thousands of displaced Lebanese residents will not be allowed to return home until northern Israel is safe, Israel's defence minister says.

item.title: ICE agents deploy to major US airports as security queues stretch for hours
item.description: [BBC News] Hundreds of ICE agents have been deployed to 14 airports, in cities including New York, Atlanta and Houston. [France 24] US immigration agency began deploying to major airports on Monday, as officials scrambled to ease mounting travel disruption during a prolonged, partial government shutdown. The move places Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) personnel – already under intense scrutiny after fatal shootings linked to immigration operations – in highly visible roles at crowded transport hubs across the country.
item.titles: ICE agents deploy to major US airports as security queues stretch for hours United States: ICE agents patrol airports amid govt shutdown

item.title: When AI 'enhances' or 'improves' an image
item.description: Using AI to "enhance" or "upscale" an image can make it look sharper. But it can also be dangerous. Several pieces of misinformation have recently circulated using these kinds of images.

item.title: In Paris, enigmatic posters spark rumours of Céline Dion concerts
item.description: While Céline Dion may have performed a showstopping song at the Paris Olympics, it has been years since the singer has performed a concert. According to Canadian media, she's planning on giving multiple shows in Paris. While no official announcement has come in yet, there are some mysterious signs popping up in Paris.

item.title: Israeli forces to control south Lebanon up to Litani river
item.description: [France 24] Israel said on March 24 that its military would take control of south Lebanon up to the Litani River, around 30 kilometres (20 miles) from the border, as deadly strikes pounded the country. The day before, Israel's finance minister had said Israel should annex the region. This comes as Israel continues to target Hezbollah targets in south Lebanon and southern suburbs of Beirut. FRANCE 24's Claire Paccalin breaks it down for us. [France 24] The Israeli Defence Forces are setting a military zone in south Lebanon of about 30 kilometres between the border and the Litani river. The military also said it had destroyed a bridge across the river overnight, which it says was used by Iran-backed Lebanese militant group Hezbollah "for transferring weapons, rockets and launchers". FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky breaks the situation down for us [France 24] Israel shows no signs of slowing down its military campaign in Lebanon. It has so far killed at least 1039 people and displaced more than a million in more than three weeks of fighting with the Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah. Instead,Tel Aviv said on March 24 its military will seize control of the area south of the Litani river. Hezbollah has vowed to fight back.
item.titles: Israeli forces to control south Lebanon up to Litani river Israel's occupation of South Lebanon 'way to pressure Lebanese government' over Hezbollah Tension rises between Israel and Lebanon as IDF occupies south of Litani river

item.title: Iran strikes put in question US-Israeli attacks's impact on military capabilities
item.description: [France 24] Iranian drone strikes battered Tel Aviv, damaging apartments and injuring several people. The attacks put into question whether Iran's military capabilities have truly been weakened by US-Israeli attacks. It has been launching sporadic strikes to preserve its arsenal while letting its enemies' stockpile run out. [Al Jazeera] Iranian missiles have caused damage in Israel, raising questions about the country’s air defence systems. [Africanews] Israel said it carried out an extensive series of strikes on Iranian “production sites,” without providing more information. Iran also fired multiple waves of missiles at Israel early on Tuesday
item.titles: Iran strikes put in question US-Israeli attacks's impact on military capabilities Israeli defences questioned as Iran continues to cause damage Israel bombs Tehran as Iran hits Telaviv, Gulf states

[Business Insider Africa] Kenyas independent fuel retailers report shortages at about 20% of stations due to disruptions from the ongoing Iran war
item.titles: Kenya fuel retailers running short of supplies amid Middle East war Kenya’s fuel retailers are running low on supply, and it could become a total crisis

Dans ce message, il affirme que cette épreuve, bien que difficile, ne remet pas en cause les valeurs, l’intégrité et le parcours de l’intéressé. Il insiste également sur le fait que ce type de situation reflète davantage les failles de ceux qui en sont à l’origine. Rama Valayden souligne par ailleurs l’engagement

item.title: In Beirut, refugees stay put despite Israeli strikes
item.description: Since the start of the war in the Middle East, over one million people in Lebanon have been displaced. In the southern suburbs of Beirut, Israeli strikes have become a daily part of life. But in this neighbourhood FRANCE 24 went to, where refugees reside, few are those who decided to leave. FRANCE 24 reports.

item.title: Djibouti: Djibouti's New Horizon - President Guelleh Unveils $480 Million 'Salaam City'
item.description: [AllAfrica] [Shabelle] Djibouti City -- H.E. Ismail Omar Guelleh, President of the Republic of Djibouti, on Monday launched a $480 million residential project dubbed "Salaam City," as Djibouti steps up efforts to meet rising housing demand driven by rapid economic growth. [AllAfrica] [Nile Post] President Ismail Omar Guelleh on Monday led the groundbreaking for Salaam City, the largest residential development in Djibouti's history, marking a significant step in the nation's urban expansion.
item.titles: Djibouti: Djibouti's New Horizon - President Guelleh Unveils $480 Million 'Salaam City' Djibouti: Djibouti Breaks Ground On Largest Residential Project in Nation's History

item.description: Delano D'Souza is pleased to welcome Scott Lucas, Professor of American Studies and International Politics at the Clinton Institute, University College Dublin. Mr. Lucas warns there has been no breakthrough whatsoever in diplomacy: An improvised and reactive process shaped by market pressures, geopolitical signalling, and misread assumptions about regime fragility is disguised as decisive action. The expectation that escalation will produce capitulation misunderstands both the resilience of the

item.title: Israel and US interests diverge on possible negociation for end of war
item.description: While U.S. President Donald Trump projects optimism that an end to the war with Iran can be negociated soon, Israel seems determined to keep up the fight. The possibility of the US ending or even withdrawing from the conflict poses a significant risk to Israel's own goals.

item.title: Australia and EU agree sweeping trade deal in face of global uncertainty
item.description: [BBC News] Australia and the EU sign sweeping trade and security deals after years of negotiations. [France 24] The European Union and Australia struck a long-awaited free-trade deal on March 24. The two countries have also agreed to boost defence cooperation and access to crucial rare-earth minerals in the face of global uncertainty over trade. Speaking while visiting Australia, EU chief Ursula von der Leyen said it was necessary at a time where tariffs and supplu chains are being weaponised. [France 24] The European Union and Australia struck a long-awaited free-trade deal on Tuesday as the bloc seeks to diversify its export markets and expand beyond ​its traditional partners. The deal, however, has drawn stern criticism from European farmers' groups that called concessions on exports of Australian beef, sugar and lamb "unacceptable".
item.titles: Australia and EU agree sweeping trade deal in face of global uncertainty EU and Australia sign free trade deal and boost defence cooperation What to know about the sweeping new EU-Australia trade agreement

item.title: Senegal fishermen bear the cost of industrial and illegal fishing
item.description: In the port town of Rufisque, fishermen who have worked in these waters for decades say industrial and illegal fleets have stripped the sea bare. With no catch and no income, young men are now making a desperate choice: to leave.

item.description: The Democratic Republic of Congo is preparing to hold the second general census in its history, more than 40 years after the previous one, conducted in 1984.

item.title: US Senate confirms Markwayne Mullin as homeland security chief
item.description: [BBC News] The US senator will now oversee the embattled department after a series of immigration enforcement blunders. [France 24] Following a 54 to 45 Senate vote, Oklahoma Senator Markwayne Mullin has been appointed the new Department of Homeland Security Secretary of the United States of America. He takes the helm of an agency in transition following the high-profile dismissal of Kristi Noem earlier in March. [Al Jazeera] Here's what we know about Markwayne Mullin, who'll oversee Trump’s contentious crackdown on migrants in the US.
item.titles: US Senate confirms Markwayne Mullin as homeland security chief Who is the new US homeland security chief Markwayne Mullin? Who is Markwayne Mullin, Trump’s new homeland security chief?

item.title: Danes vote as PM Mette Frederiksen seeks third term after Greenland boost
item.description: [BBC News] Frederiksen enjoyed a surge in popularity for standing up to the US over Greenland but her support is down on 2022. [France 24] Danish PM Mette Frederiksen is seeking a third term as elections take place on March 24. She called the election in February - several months before she had to in apparent hopes that her resolute image in the crisis over Greenland would help her with voters. But the campaign was more aobut domestic issues than Denmark's relationship with Trump. FRANCE 24's Angela Diffley tells us more. [France 24] Danes voted on March 24 in parliamentary election. It could hand ​Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen a third term thanks to her staunch line against Donald Trump over Greenland, but she could also lose support over cost-of-living worries. FRANCE 24's Trine Villemann tells us what to expect from this election.
item.titles: Danes vote as PM Mette Frederiksen seeks third term after Greenland boost Danish PM seeking third term as election underway Denmark election dominated by domestic issues followed by Greenland crisis

item.title: Ultimatums, diplomacy and a trip to Graceland as Trump eyes a deal with Iran
item.description: [BBC News] America may be a nation at war, but the president's activities have been a mix of diplomacy and diversions - with the occasional swing toward the surreal. [France 24] Hopes rose that the Iran war could begin winding down as U.S. President Donald Trump said that his government was holding productive talks with Tehran. Although this was denied by Iran, it is a further sign that the US is turning towards diplomacy to end a conflict that could drag on. FRANCE 24's Philip Turle breaks it down for us. [France 24] It's day 25 of the US and Israel's war with Iran, and US President Donald Trump claims that "very good" talks are underway to end combat. He said that because of this, the US will hold off on striking Iranian power plants. But Iranian officials have contradicted this - downplaying Trump's threats and saying he's actually backing down. [France 24] US President Donald Trump on Monday walked back his own deadline for escalating attacks on Iran, citing "productive" talks with Tehran – but Iranian officials deny those talks took place. So what is really happening? Here is a look at the facts and speculation. [Al Jazeera] Iranian leaders, including Ghalibaf, have denied that talks with the US are under way. [Africanews] President Donald Trump said Monday the U.S. was talking with a “top" and "respected” Iranian leader and claimed the Islamic Republic was eager for a deal to end the war.
item.sources: BBC News France 24 Al Jazeera Africanews
item.titles: Ultimatums, diplomacy and a trip to Graceland as Trump eyes a deal with Iran US 'shifts' towards diplomacy with Iran ahead of midterms What do we know about the negotiations between the US and Iran? Trump's supposed talks with Iran: What do we know? Is the US talking to Iran’s Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, and who is he? Trump claims ‘very strong’ Iran talks as Tehran denies contact

item.title: TV series show: 'One Piece' rides again
item.description: The manga megahit "One Piece" was a global hit when the first season of its small screen adaptation was released on Netflix in 2023; TV critic Dheepthika Laurent tells us why the second season is set for similar success. We also discuss Steve Carrell's comic turn in "Rooster", a new HBO comedy set on a college campus from the makers of "Ted Lasso".

item.title: In US, thousands of Cuban migrants who arrived legally fear deportation
item.description: Cubans, who have historically received preferential treatment from the US, have not been spared by President Donald Trump's war on immigration. The US has ended family reunification and legal immigration from Cuba by adding the island to its travel ban. It has also suspended temporary protection programmes, exposing thousands of people who arrived legally to deportation to a country suffering from severe US sanctions and crippling electricity blackouts.

item.title: March shoulder season in Europe and Asia: Fewer crowds – more opportunities

While some tourists organize trips during the peak season and complain about crowded streets, others take full advantage of the March shoulder season. During this period, flights and hotels are usually more affordable, queues are almost nonexistent, and cities live in their usual rhythm without the overwhelming tourist crowds.

item.description: Le rapport de l’Audit 2024-2025 fait un constat sans concession du système pénitentiaire.
